corpus_binding.interpretation: The accepted LSLC-001A character-data case remains a public-documentation specification role with null oracle and candidate evidence. This overlay records only independently authored local Rust character-data representation tests.

validated_value_binding.relationship: XmlCharacterData::encode borrows an already validated XmlText without consuming, mutating, reinterpreting, or revalidating it.

candidate_policy.interpretation: Greater-than escaping is a conservative global Rusty LSL candidate choice, not an observation or compatibility claim.

does_not_prove: observed liblsl character-data or greater-than escaping behavior
XML element, attribute, declaration, comment, processing-instruction, CDATA-section, parser, decoder, generic entity engine, or complete document behavior
LSL stream-info field mapping, element order, empty-element form, numeric or channel-format spelling, or exact endpoint bytes
official liblsl behavior, an oracle observation, or candidate differential evidence
protocol, wire, discovery, transport, clock, inlet, outlet, runtime, adapter, provider, FFI, device, or Morphospace authority behavior
